title: These Stocks Hit 2023 Highs As Dow Heads For Best 2-Month Stretch Since November
text: A slew of strong economic and earnings data points this week “could be the catalyst to send the market to new highs,” according to one strategist.

title: Intuit to Announce Fourth-quarter and Full-year Fiscal 2023 Results on Aug. 24; Investor Day Set for Sept. 28
text: MOUNTAIN VIEW, Calif.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Intuit Inc. (Nasdaq: INTU), the global financial technology platform that makes TurboTax, Credit Karma, QuickBooks, and Mailchimp, will announce its fourth-quarter and full-year financial results for fiscal year 2023 on August 24, following the close of market. The company's fourth quarter ends on July 31. Intuit executives will discuss the financial results on a conference call at 1:30 p.m. Pacific time on August 24. The conference call can be heard live.

title: Stocks This Week: Buy Intuit And Sell Short Intuitive Surgical
text: Over the near term, the indices are likely to move higher, but an autumn correction looms. Here are a pair of stock recommendations, one long and one short.

title: Our Top 10 Picks For Dividend Growth Stocks - July 2023
text: This monthly series of articles focuses on DGI stocks that are likely to provide a high rate of dividend growth rather than a high current yield. We use our proprietary models to rate quantitatively and qualitatively and select the top ten names from an initial list of nearly 400 dividend stocks. A balanced portfolio should have a mix of high-dividend growth stocks and high-yield dividend stocks, depending on the life stage. If you are in the accumulation phase and are several years away from retirement, high-growth dividend stocks would be more appropriate for long-term growth.

title: Intuit (INTU) Introduces QuickBooks Workforce Application
text: Intuit (INTU) introduces QuickBooks Workforce mobile app to automate and reduce administrative tasks of business employees in the United States, Canada, Australia and U.K.

title: Intuit's Compounding Magic: A Tech Investor's Dream
text: Intuit is a strong choice for dividend growth investors seeking exposure to the technology sector, with consistent double-digit annual returns since 1994. Despite a low yield of 0.6%, INTU's dividend has grown at a rate of 14.9% per year over the past five years, indicating its ongoing growth trajectory.

title: Intuit Launches New QuickBooks Workforce Mobile App
text: MOUNTAIN VIEW, Calif.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Employees of small and larger businesses often need to use disconnected tools to track their work hours, schedules, and paid time off, leaving them frustrated and uncertain about finances on payday and between paychecks. Today, Intuit Inc. (Nasdaq: INTU) alleviates these pain points on a global scale with the launch of the new QuickBooks Workforce mobile app. Intuit is the global financial technology platform that makes Intuit TurboTax, Credit Karma, Quic.

title: Tax Prep Companies May Have Shared Sensitive Data With Big Tech For Years
text: Tax preparation firms like H&R Block have spent years sharing taxpayers' sensitive personal information with big tech companies including Google parent Alphabet and Meta Platforms.

title: Best Financial Stocks to Buy
text: Investors seeking out the best financial stocks will want to tread carefully, focusing on high-quality names with solid fundamentals.
